breaking_changes.detail: 609 of 1,004 stop_ids from the old feed are absent in the new feed. These are entrance/zone nodes whose ID separator changed (e.g. 'AG-A' → 'AG:A', 'LH-A' → 'LH:A'), so this is simultaneously a primary-key rename, not a clean deletion.
breaking_changes.note: CRITICAL TO VERIFY (and verified clean): stop_times.txt references only the 286 platform-level boarding stops (e.g. 'AG_L5_V1'), whose IDs were NOT changed and exist in both feeds. So despite 609 IDs disappearing, no stop_times foreign key is broken. The renamed entities are accessibility/entrance nodes consumed by pathways.txt, not the schedule.

breaking_changes.detail: Entrance and zone stop_ids switched separator from hyphen to colon ('-A'/'-ZP'/'-ZNP' → ':A' etc.). Any external system that hard-referenced the old entrance IDs (deep links, saved trips, third-party apps) breaks.
breaking_changes.note: Internal referential integrity is intact — parent_station chains for all schedule stops resolve, and the new entrance IDs are matched by pathways.txt.

suspicious_changes.detail: Stop count grew 1,004 → 6,275 (+525%), far exceeding the 20% threshold. Driven by 5,880 added stops: elevator/escalator/pathway nodes ('AG:ASC01_BOT', 'AG:ESCN01_TOP') introduced alongside the new pathways.txt and levels.txt files.
suspicious_changes.note: Consistent and intentional — an accessibility-data expansion, not a data error. But it trips the count-delta rule hard.

suspicious_changes.detail: Unique shape_id count dropped 61 → 14 (-77%), with only 14 in common; file shrank 110,777 → 27,764 bytes. Geometry continuity for the 47 dropped shapes cannot be verified.
suspicious_changes.note: All trips.shape_id values still resolve against the 14 remaining shapes — the schedule lost no references — but a 77% reduction in shape variety is a large geometry change worth flagging.

checks_passed.removed_required_field: none — stops.txt dropped stop_code and stop_url, both optional; new feed adds level_id, levels.txt, pathways.txt
checks_passed.removed_file: none — 2 files added (levels.txt, pathways.txt), 0 removed. Note: old feed was nested under a gtfs_v4/ directory, new feed is flat — a packaging change, not a content change
checks_passed.changed_field_type_or_format: none; all 246,099 stop_times rows have valid HH:MM:SS times
checks_passed.referential_integrity_new_feed: clean — stop_times→stops (0 missing), stop_times→trips (0 missing), trips→shapes (0 missing), trips→routes/service (0 missing), stops→parent_station (0 missing), pathways→stops (0 missing), stops→levels (0 missing)
checks_passed.enum_values: identical: route_type {1}, pickup_type {0,1}, drop_off_type {0}, timepoint {0}, exception_type {1,2}, direction_id {0,1}
checks_passed.entity_count_deltas: trips 0%, stop_times 0%, routes 0%, calendar 0% — only stops (+525%) and shapes (-77%) exceed 20%
checks_passed.geographic_bbox: effectively unchanged: [-33.60978, -70.7573]→[-33.36515, -70.54491] vs new max-lon -70.54439 (~50m, within noise)
checks_passed.service_coverage_gap: none — identical service window, full overlap
checks_passed.headsign_route_name_bulk_change: 0% — route names and 100% of common-trip headsigns unchanged
checks_passed.calendar_flip: no — both use calendar.txt + calendar_dates.txt with identical service definitions
